meaning: n.惠特尼(m.)  ,Mount 惠特尼山[美国加利福尼亚州东部]
examples: Whitney blinked and emitted a long, low whistle.
She writes a lot of contemporary music for people like Whitney Houston.
A survey I conducted with Irene Whitney found that in British primary schools up to a quarter of pupils reported experience of bullying, which in about one in ten cases was persistent.
When asked about a Whitney hologram in late 2014, Pat Houston expressed mixed feelings.
One potentially alarming trend is banks pulling back consumer credit lines, says Oppenheimer analyst Meredith Whitney.
